title: Add data tests to a dbt model
description: Steps to attach uniqueness, not-null, relationship, and freshness tests so bad data fails the build, not the dashboard.
body: Use when a model lacks tests and feeds consumers. Do not rely on spot-checking output — silent failures surface as wrong dashboards days later. Actions: declare unique and not-null tests on the primary key, relationship tests to parent dimensions, accepted-values tests on enums, and source-freshness checks; wire them into CI and scheduled runs. Validation signals include a CI run executing the tests and a build that fails on seeded duplicate or orphaned keys. Risk boundary: an untested model lets a broken join propagate warehouse-wide; if key and relationship tests are missing, treat the model as unsafe to publish and add them before exposing it to consumers.
